---
title: "Our first Quarto example"
author: "Pozdniakov"
format: html
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to ***weave*** together content and executable code into a *finished document* [^1]. To **learn more** about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

[^1]: Это сноска

This will be NOT calculated: `2+2`.\
This will be calculated: `r 2+2`

```{r import_data_and_packages}
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(leaflet)
library(DT)
penguins <- palmerpenguins::penguins
```

```{r}
#| warning: false
as.numeric(c(1, "dva"))
```

## Картиночка

![quarto scheme](Louis-DelphinOdobeyCadet.jpg)

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}

1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: true
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

## {plotly}

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
g_penguins <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(data = penguins, 
             aes(x = flipper_length_mm,
                 y = body_mass_g,
                 size = body_mass_g,
                 shape = sex,
                 colour = body_mass_g),
             alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(data = penguins, 
              aes(x = flipper_length_mm,
                  y = body_mass_g),
              colour = "#202030", method = "lm") +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(option = "inferno", end = .9) +
  facet_wrap(~island) +
  labs(x = "Flipper length, mm",
       y = "Body mass, g",
       title = "Penguins",
       subtitle = "...but not from Madagaskar",
       caption = "Pozdniakov Ivan",
       colour = "Body mass, g",
       size = "Body mass, g",
       shape = "Sex") +
  hrbrthemes::theme_ipsum()
ggplotly(g_penguins)
```

## {leaflet}

```{r}

leaflet() %>%
  addTiles() %>%
  addMarkers(lat=55.767290, lng=37.664066, popup="Our academic home")

```

## {DT}

```{r}
datatable(penguins)
```
